channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Es inoponible la cláusula de carencia de 90 días en un seguro de Vida por infarto de miocardio
item.description: La Sala Primera del Tribunal Supremo ha resuelto que la cláusula que establece un período de carencia de 90 días para la cobertura del infarto de miocardio en un seguro de Vida temporal constituye una cláusula limitativa de los derechos del asegurado y no una mera delimitación del riesgo. Al no cumplir los requisitos formales del artículo 3 de la Ley de Contrato de Seguro (LCS), la cláusula resulta inoponible al asegurado. En una reciente sentencia, comentada en Revista RC , el Supremo reitera la doctrina fijada en la sentencia 1321/2023, de 27 de septiembre, sobre cláusulas de carencia en seguros de Vida.

item.title: Compliance en seguros: valor estratégico en un entorno regulado
item.description: Desde mi experiencia, el verdadero valor de Compliance no reside únicamente en supervisar el cumplimiento normativo. Mantenerla exige solvencia financiera, excelencia operativa y, cada vez más, una gobernanza sólida capaz de responder a un entorno regulatorio en constante evolución. En este contexto, la función de Compliance ha adquirido un papel central dentro de las entidades aseguradoras .

item.title: Olga Sánchez: «Axa se encuentra en la mejor posición que hemos tenido nunca»
item.description: Asimismo, señaló que la aseguradora se encuentra «en la mejor posición que hemos tenido nunca», gracias a «una visión estratégica clara, las mejores herramientas y personas que hacen que la compañía avance con una clara ejecución de los proyectos». El acto contó también con la participación de Olga Sánchez, consejera delegada de Axa España, quien intervino en una mesa redonda junto a todos los miembros del Comité Ejecutivo de la compañía. En este sentido, Mar Romero, directora de Distribución, Ventas y Organización Territorial (en la imagen que acompaña a esta información) , destacó la fortaleza de la estructura comercial: « Tenemos el mejor equipo comercial, la mejor red y los mejores productos «.

item.title: Mutual of Enumclaw Names Peterson Chief Claims Officer
item.description: Mutual of Enumclaw named Joe Peterson vice president of claims and chief claims officer. Peterson has more than 20 years of claims leadership experience, most recently as vice president – P/C claims at Zenith Insurance Company. Mutual of Enumclaw is a Washington-based regional insurer specializing in personal, business and farm and ranch coverage.

item.title: Wall Street Watchdogs Pause Some Cyber Exams After Mythos Shock
item.description: In April, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and then-Fed Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to warn them of the cyber risks raised by AI models such as Mythos. regulators are pausing some cyber-related examinations of the largest banks, giving the lenders more breathing room as the firms dig into the risks exposed by Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model. Anthropic said last month that it would limit access to Mythos, warning that it could potentially be used to power cyber attacks.

item.title: Openly’s Tech-Forward Approach Includes AI in Claims
item.description: Openly, which promotes itself as a tech-forward homeowners insurance provider, has been making a name for itself by enabling agents to generate quotes in times as little as 15 seconds since being founded in 2017. The Boston, Massachusetts-based insurtech, which operates in 19 states and counting, continues to embrace that tech-forward mantra by adopting artificial intelligence in its claims process. To get a good handle what the company is doing with AI, Claims Journal spoke with April Stone, senior director of claims at Openly.

item.title: Aon Malaysia confirms double leadership promotions
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Zurich elevates Kin Lee to APAC head of energy
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: MS Amlin posts 30% jump in insurance service result, COR down to 83%
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Young drivers warned about fake insurance sold on social media
item.description: Half (49%) of young drivers have bought insurance through social media or messaging apps, new research reveals. The FCA is warning 17-to 25-year-old drivers about 'ghost broking' scams where criminals sell bogus insurance policies through social media and messaging platforms. Check the FCA Firm Checker before you buy, because driving uninsured could cost you far more than any premium.' The FCA is working with social media influencers to warn young drivers about the growing threat of ghost broking.

item.title: Travelers secures its largest cat bond ever, $750m Long Point Re IV 2026-1 priced
item.description: The Travelers Companies, the US property & casualty insurance giant, has now successfully priced what will be its largest catastrophe bond sponsorship ever, securing $750 million of multi-peril reinsurance protection from the new Long Point Re IV Ltd. Now, we’re told that Travelers has secured the upper-end target for $750 million of collateralized catastrophe reinsurance protection from this new Long Point Re VI Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond sponsorship. This new Series 2026-1 cat bond also becomes the largest ever sponsored by Travelers, which is a notable fact when you consider the stature of the insurer and the fact it continues to introduce more capital markets backed reinsurance to its tower.

item.title: TWIA’s fully-committed $2.28bn reinsurance renewal the “optimal structure at the lowest price”
item.description: The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association’s (TWIA) reinsurance renewal for 2026 is fully-committed by markets and expected to be signed in the next couple of days, while Allen Cashin of Gallagher Re said that the competitive auction-like process the broker employed has resulted in the “optimal structure at the lowest price.” As we’d reported back in February, in order to meet its new 1-in-50 year probable maximum loss funding level for 2026 TWIA only requires around $2.28 billion of risk transfer in total this year . As a result, TWIA will have $1.05 billion of its roughly $2.28 billion of risk transfer and reinsurance need supplied by the cat bond market in 2026, while a further roughly $1.23 billion of reinsurance will be provided in traditional form, although perhaps some being from collateralized or fronted ILS markets we expect. At a Board meeting yesterday, TWIA’s leadership heard from its broker Gallagher Re, which has been placing the new cat bonds reinsurance for the insurer.

item.title: Mereo enters excess casualty insurance with Everen renewal rights acquisition
item.description: Bermuda-based carrier Mereo Insurance Limited (Mereo) has expanded into the excess casualty insurance space with the acquisition of Everen Specialty Ltd.’s (ESL) excess casualty energy portfolio renewal rights ESL is a member of the Everen Group and a Bermuda-domiciled global insurer for energy markets. Additionally, ESL’s underwriting team and underwriting systems handling the excess casualty energy portfolio will transition to Mereo, and be led by former ESL Chief Underwriting Officer, Carla Greaves. As per the terms of the transaction, Mereo will become the insurance carrier on a go-forward basis for both new and renewed business effective June 1st, 2026.

item.title: Underwriting and investment performance lift MS Re’s profit to $415m for 2025
item.description: Robert Wiest, Chief Executive Officer of MS Reinsurance, commented, “2025 was an excellent year for the company as we delivered a significantly increased net profit of $415 million, based on excellent underwriting and investment performance. MS Reinsurance, the Switzerland-domiciled global reinsurer, has revealed that 2025 was another strong year for the firm, with net profit after tax reaching $415 million, up from $346 million in 2024. According to MS Re, a wholly owned subsidiary of MSI, which is a core part of the Japanese insurance giant MS&AD Group, the strong net income figure for the year ended 2025 was driven by significant positive contributions from both underwriting and investments.

item.title: European reinsurers deliver higher profits despite weaker revenue growth in Q1’26: Fitch
item.description: Fitch Ratings, the international credit ratings and research agency, has said that Europe’s four largest reinsurers posted stronger earnings in the first quarter of 2026, although overall revenue growth declined as market conditions became less favourable. Fitch Ratings reported that combined revenue across the four reinsurers fell by approximately 5% year-on-year. Despite softer pricing conditions, Fitch Ratings said the four reinsurers remain in a strong position to achieve their profitability objectives for 2026.

item.title: Man Wrongly Convicted of Killing 2 Michigan Hunters Agrees to $5M Settlement
item.description: A man who spent nearly 21 years in prison for the deaths of two Michigan hunters agreed to a $5.25 million settlement after accusing police of failing to turn over evidence that could have helped him at trial, a lawyer said Monday. The Innocence Clinic at University of Michigan law school and two investigators got authorities to acknowledge that an Ohio serial killer might have been the person who killed the hunters in 1990. Students and staff at University of Michigan law school were trying to get him a new trial when a 30-page file from the original investigation was discovered at the county sheriff’s office.

item.title: Death at SpaceX’s South Texas Facilities Prompts Workplace Safety Investigation
item.description: The Occupational Safety and Health Administration is investigating an incident at SpaceX’s South Texas facilities that resulted in the death of a worker. “SpaceX’s operations and workplace matters are not within the City’s purview,” she added. While OSHA confirmed it was investigating the incident, the workplace safety agency did not release more details.

item.title: Michigan Secures Settlement With Car Financing Company Over Auto-Repair Agreements
item.description: The settlement will primarily be used to provide restitution to eligible Michigan consumers who entered into auto-repair lease-to-own agreements with the company. Within 45 days, the company is also required to ensure that lease-to-own agreements are not used to pay for auto-repair-related parts and services. Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel has secured an agreement with West Creek Financial, Inc., a Virginia corporation doing business under the name Koalafi, to resolve allegations that the lease-to-own financing company violated the Michigan Consumer Protection Act and Rental-Purchase Agreement Act.

item.title: Lilypad Names Matta Chief Innovation Officer
item.description: (Image source: Lilypad Insurance homepage.) Lilypad Insurance (New York), an admitted property/casualty insurance group focused on coastal property risk, has appointed Rajiv Matta Chief Innovation Officer. Matta joins Lilypad from Millennial Specialty Insurance, a technology-driven MGA within The Baldwin Group , where he served as Chief Innovation Officer. Rajiv Matta, Chief Innovation Officer, Lilypad Insurance.

item.title: Stephen Applebaum Remembered
item.description: With great sorrow, we note the passing of our friend Stephen Applebaum. Readers will know Stephen as a regular contributor to IIR, often writing with his collaborator Alan Demers. Stephen’s authority came from long experience as a builder, operator, analyst and advisor in the claims technology ecosystem.

item.title: Asia’s ‘interlinked’ protection gaps require a virtuous cycle of risk reduction, insurance, and government backing: GAIP
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints Tokio Marine veteran Max Hirai as Japan CEO
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Jeeves refuerza los pagos transfronterizos en Colombia, la más rezagada en la región
item.description: La plataforma de finanzas empresariales Jeeves desplegó una línea de crédito de US$ 100 millones para impulsar sus operaciones de pagos transfronterizos en América Latina y Colombia, con uno de los mayores rezagos en la región, estará entre sus mercados clave este año. La compañía ampliará su oferta en el segmento B2B con tres líneas de servicio: cuentas globales, automatización de pagos y tesorería y tarjetas virtuales especializadas por vertical. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Definity Adopts Earnix to Modernize Pricing
item.description: Definity (Waterloo, Ontario), a Canadian property/casualty insurer, has selected Earnix (Ramat Gan, Israel) to accelerate pricing decisions and improve customer response times across its personal auto, property and individually rated commercial auto (IRCA) portfolios. Definity aims to modernize its pricing infrastructure as pricing complexity increases across lines of business and as the company seeks to respond more quickly to market changes. We do this with confidence, knowing the built-in governance and controls ensure our pricing decisions are transparent, auditable, and fully compliant.” Definity selected Earnix following an evaluation of end-to-end pricing solutions for personal auto, property and IRCA.

item.title: U.S. Coasts Could See Double Whammy With El Niño, High Tide Flooding
item.description: With a possible El Niño developing between now and December, the chances of high-tide flooding impacts could be even stronger, NOAA researchers said. “It usually ends up being a double whammy,” said NOAA oceanographer and high tide flooding expert William Sweet, Ph.D, noting that the especially strong El Niños in 2015-2016 and 2023-2024 brought much more frequent flooding. And now with this second punch – a strong El Niño – coastal communities face more frequent, deeper, and widespread high tide flooding along both the West and East Coasts.”

item.title: Waymo Recalls 3,800 Robotaxis Over Risk of Entering Flooded Roads
item.description: Waymo said on Tuesday it is recalling about 3,800 robotaxis in the United States after identifying a risk that vehicles could enter flooded roads with higher speed limits, raising safety concerns. Waymo said the vehicle was unoccupied, and there were no injuries, but the incident prompted the company to review similar scenarios involving high speeds and impassable flooded roads. The Alphabet unit said the recall followed an incident on April 20 when a Waymo vehicle drove into a flooded lane in San Antonio during extreme weather.

item.title: NICB Warns of Rising Contractor Fraud During Awareness Week
item.description: Throughout Contractor Fraud Awareness Week, NICB has also launched a public education campaign offering resources to consumers in collaboration with NICB members, AARP, the Insurance Information Institute, and the Coalition Against Insurance Fraud. To recognize the sixth annual Contractor Fraud Awareness Week held between May 18 and 22, the National Insurance Crime Bureau is reminding homeowners and businesses to remain vigilant about the growing threat of contractor fraud following natural disasters and severe weather events. As disaster damage continues to rise across the country, Americans must remain alert to the warning signs of contractor fraud.” After six years of Contractor Fraud Awareness Week, a total of 36 states support the education of hard-hit communities about the financial harm that unscrupulous storm-chasers commit after natural disasters.

item.title: Favourable Mountain Re 2026-1 cat bond execution reflects carrier strength: Hippo’s McCathron
item.description: (Series 2026-1) issuance, reflects parent company Hippo Holdings’ ability to return to the capital markets and execute at favourable terms, according to Rick McCathron, President and CEO of Hippo Holdings. Rick McCathron, President and CEO of Hippo Holdings, commented: “Our ability to return to the capital markets and execute at favorable terms is a direct reflection of the strength of our carrier platform. This Mountain Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond is set to provide Spinnaker Insurance with reinsurance on a per-occurrence and indemnity trigger basis that runs across a three-year term, to June 7th, 2029.

item.title: Bermuda Stock Exchange cat bond and ILS listings soar in Q1, growing its market share
item.description: Listings of catastrophe bonds and other insurance-linked securities (ILS) soared in the first-quarter of 2026 for the Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) and it further grew its market share, as total cat bond and ILS listings on the BSX reached over $68.5 billion. The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) continues to cement its position as the dominant venue for listings of catastrophe bonds and other ILS structures. Through 2025 the BSX expanded its market share as its cat bond and ILS listings reached $65.2 billion, with just over 93% of the cat bond market listed there as of December 31st .

item.title: MNRB’s Labuan Re acquisition to strengthen reinsurance focus and boost international growth
item.description: The acquisition of Labuan Re strengthens MNRB’s international reinsurance growth and aligns its global competitiveness and optimises capital deployment across its reinsurance and retakaful operations. Labuan Re has built a credible presence in the international reinsurance market, and we believe that bringing it fully within MNRB will create a stronger platform for future growth. Commenting on the proposed acquisition, Dato’ Sulaiman Mohd Tahir, Chairman of MNRB, said: “This acquisition is a meaningful step in MNRB’s growth journey and reflects our confidence in the long-term potential of Labuan Re as part of the Group.

item.title: Reinsurance Association of America names Donegan as SVP, Head of State Relations
item.description: Reinsurance Association of America (RAA), a trade association for property and casualty reinsurance in the United States, has appointed Susan Donegan as Senior Vice President (SVP), Head of State Relations, effective June 1st, 2026. Donegan is an industry veteran and was most recently serving as Chief Regulatory Officer at the National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI), where she led engagement with 36 state insurance departments and legislatures, as well as federal agencies and industry organisations. As Vermont Commissioner, Donegan also served on several committees of the National Association of Insurance Commissioners and the International Association of Insurance Supervisors.

item.title: Hippo expands multi-peril coverage to include wildfire with latest catastrophe bond
item.description: “It was a pleasure to advise Hippo on its second catastrophe bond and help expand its reinsurance protection to include wildfire — a critical coverage in today’s environment.” This transaction is Hippo’s second catastrophe bond issuance, following its $110 million debut offering in 2023. (Series 2026-1), a $100 million catastrophe bond which provides multi-year collateralized reinsurance protection against a broad range of U.S. The addition of fire, primarily targeting California wildfire exposure, is a new feature for this issuance and highlights Hippo’s commitment to maintaining reinsurance protection that adapts to the evolving risk landscape.

item.title: Missouri AG Resolves Litigation with Gas Company After Fatal Explosion
item.description: Missouri Attorney General Catherine Hanaway announced that her office has resolved litigation with Liberty Utilities, a gas company that failed to properly mark a gas line in Lexington, leading to the death of a child, numerous injuries, and the destruction of a home. The State’s litigation, separate from any private actions filed by the victims of the explosion, concluded with a consent judgment requiring Liberty Utilities to pay the maximum civil penalty of $30,000 for violating the Missouri Underground Facility Safety and Damage Prevention Act and to engage in a remapping process of their gas lines to prevent future tragedies. The company must also verify and remap all underground facilities it owns or operates within Lexington, Missouri.

item.title: Lilypad Leans Into AI for Coastal Areas with Matta Hire
item.description: Lilypad, the Sarasota-based, tech-centered property-casualty insurer that acquired Centauri Insurance, has named Rajiv Matta, a holder of patents in the field of machine learning, to harness artificial intelligence to help expand coverage in vulnerable coastal areas. Matta, with 20 years of experience with large carriers and reinsurers, will be chief innovation officer at Lilypad, the company announced. “Lilypad is built on a conviction I’ve held for years: that AI isn’t a feature you add to an insurance company, it’s the foundation you build on,” Matta said in a news release.

item.title: Zurich expands AI underwriting rollout with Cytora across global commercial business
item.description: The post Zurich expands AI underwriting rollout with Cytora across global commercial business appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Zurich Insurance Group has expanded its AI-driven underwriting transformation through a partnership with Cytora, deploying Cytora’s risk digitisation platform across five countries within 90 days and significantly reducing manual underwriting effort. The insurer plans to extend the rollout to more than 20 markets over the next 16 months as it scales agentic AI capabilities across its commercial insurance operations.

item.title: Everest to sell Colombia insurance unit to AIG as portfolio refocus continues
item.description: Everest Group has signed a definitive agreement to sell its Colombia insurance subsidiary, Everest Compañía de Seguros Generales Colombia S.A., to American International Group. The transaction continues Everest’s broader portfolio optimisation strategy and follows its previously announced sale of global Commercial Retail Insurance renewal rights to AIG, as well as the divestment of its Canada Retail Insurance operations. Everest said the move supports its focus on strengthening its core Global Reinsurance and Wholesale & Specialty Insurance businesses, where it sees the strongest long-term growth opportunities and competitive advantages.

item.title: Prudential launches AI assistant Just Ask for advisors and distributors
item.description: Prudential Financial has introduced Just Ask , an AI-powered assistant designed to help financial professionals quickly access information related to Prudential products, underwriting quotes, and new business forms. The tool is now available to Prudential’s third-party advisors and active distributors, consolidating frequently used information into a single interface. Instead of navigating multiple systems, users can ask questions directly and receive responses specific to Prudential Individual Life products and processes.

item.title: Prudential introduces Just Ask
item.description: Prudential Financial is introducing Just Ask, an AI‑powered tool designed to help financial professionals get answers about Prudential products, quotes and new business forms. Now available to Prudential’s third‑party advisors and active distributors, Just Ask brings commonly requested information into one place. We built Just Ask to support those moments, especially around underwriting, where timing matters.” – Simon Berg, technology business leader for Prudential Individual Life Insurance.

item.title: HawkSoft and RevitPay Launch Direct API Payments Integration
item.description: Through its direct API integration, RevitPay processes payments directly in the HawkSoft AMS, helping agencies accelerate premium collection, automate reconciliation, and improve cash flow visibility. “Through this partnership with HawkSoft, we are building on years of experience supporting insurance agencies by delivering expanded resources, modern payment capabilities, and a direct API integration designed to help agencies collect premiums faster and operate more efficiently within HawkSoft.” Coley’s leadership brings continuity for agencies familiar with ePaymints while introducing enhanced functionality and long-term support through RevitPay’s integrated payments platform. RevitPay’s direct API integration means payments, reconciliation, and cash flow are fluid experiences with data in HawkSoft, where agencies are already working.” Agencies interested in learning more can visit www.hawksoft.com/partners/revitpay .

item.title: Aviva Q1 premiums rise 19%
item.description: Aviva reported Q1 2026 general insurance premiums of £3.4 billion, up 19% year over year, while the group combined operating ratio improved to 94.1%. Aviva also reaffirmed its 2026 guidance and said it expects to remain within its target solvency range after delivering planned Direct Line capital synergies. We delivered profitable growth across Aviva despite global market volatility, demonstrating yet again the advantages of our market-leading positions and diverse business model.

item.title: New York Homes Most Exposed to Hurricane Risk, Beating Miami
item.description: Houston and Miami rank second and third at risk, according to the number of homes that are exposed to hurricane wind. More than 3 million New York metropolitan-area homes are vulnerable to hurricane winds, the most of any region in the country, according to a report from Cotality, a property analytics firm. At the state level, Florida ranks first with the country’s largest concentration of homes at risk of hurricane-wind damage, followed by Texas, North Carolina, New York and New Jersey, Cotality said.

item.title: Why and How Homeowners Underwriting Must Change
item.description: Excellence in homeowners insurance requires sophisticated risk management, but today’s industry environment is testing the limits of traditional underwriting approaches. These elements compound to make underwriting homeowners insurance more complex, dynamic and more dependent on real-time information than ever before. Here, he discusses how the changing nature of risk is forcing a new approach to underwriting—one that requires underwriters to obtain a more holistic, end-to-end view of the insurance life cycle and to discover more effective uses of existing data.

item.title: Insurance adviser Pro Global expands into APAC with Australian acquisition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Seadrif taps Richard Poulter to lead sovereign asset and fiscal empowerment facility
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Malaysian Re parent takes over Labuan Re in US$100m deal
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Venture Capital Is Concentrating Faster Than Ever. What Happens To Everyone Else?
item.description: venture capital in 2025, a total of $88 billion in rounds starting anywhere from $1 million to less than $100 million. venture capital concentration on record, with investment clustering in top private companies even more so than it did in the previous peak year of 2021, when startup funding more than doubled year over year . venture capital totals in 2026 are on par with funding for all of 2025, and 80% of startup investment this year so far has gone to rounds of $500 million and more, across 29 companies.

item.title: Mangrove lowers pricing again for debut Buttonwood Re cat bond
item.description: Mangrove Property Insurance continues to target between $100 million and $111 million of named storm reinsurance protection from its debut Buttonwood Re Ltd. For its first catastrophe bond sponsorship under Buttonwood Re, Mangrove is seeking to secure both occurrence and aggregate reinsurance to protect its business against losses from named storms and hurricanes in the state of Florida. Initially, Mangrove was targeting $100 million of reinsurance across issuance of four tranches of Series 2026-1 cat bond notes by Buttonwood Re, that will be sold to investors and the proceeds used to collateralize reinsurance agreements with the cedent, Mangrove.

item.title: Allstate focuses on four-year Florida reinsurance from $200m Sanders Re III 2026-2 cat bond
item.description: Allstate is now focused on securing a $200 million four year source of Florida catastrophe reinsurance protection with its latest catastrophe bond, as a three year tranche of notes has now been dropped from this Sanders Re III Ltd. is now offering a single $200 million tranche of Class A-2 Series 2026-2 notes, to provide Allstate with a four year source of fully-collateralized reinsurance, on a cascading indemnity and per-occurrence trigger basis. US primary insurer Allstate returned to the catastrophe bond market earlier this month, seeking its typical May sponsorship of a cat bond to provide it with multi-peril Florida collateralized reinsurance protection.

item.title: PwC expects deeper ILS integration and greater alignment of interests: Arthur Wightman
item.description: As the insurance-linked securities (ILS) market persistently acts as a moderating force, helping to create a more managed reinsurance cycle, PwC expects deeper integration and greater alignment of interests between alternative capital and rated balance sheets, according to Arthur Wightman, Territory Leader of PwC Bermuda. Wightman also told Artemis that PwC expects there to be a greater alignment of interest between ILS and traditional reinsurance companies. “The Bermuda market has really embraced ILS, so we would at PwC expect to see much deeper integration, not displacement.

item.title: US personal motor and home insurance rate changes ease towards pre-pandemic patterns in 2025: AM Best
item.description: AM Best, the credit rating agency, has reported that average annual rate increases for US private passenger motor and homeowners insurance in 2025 have moved closer to levels seen before the pandemic period. Despite the national slowdown in rate increases, AM Best points out that states including California, Nevada, New Jersey and New York continued to show comparatively elevated motor insurance pricing in 2025. AM Best’s data shows that the average approved rise for homeowners insurance declined by 5.2 percentage points year on year to 8.3% in 2025, while private passenger motor insurance recorded an average approved increase of 3.7%, down from 9.7% in 2024.
